response.justification: Bogdan carries genuine Staff-level credentials, direct hands-on experience building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure, and a demonstrated history of high-throughput distributed systems work. However, Python — a listed must-have — is entirely absent from the skills section, every role description, and all side projects, which is a material gap. The distributed systems depth is real but concentrated in roles ending around 2006, and the current re:cinq work reads more as full-stack product and tooling than large-scale infrastructure ownership. A short screening call to probe Python exposure and current architectural scope is warranted before a firm decision.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) maps directly to the AI-native engineering baseline the role treats as a given
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design

Long Staff-level IC career with strong TypeScript, software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD), and a track record of technical knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume — skills list, all role descriptions, and every side project — despite being listed as a must-have technical skill

High-throughput distributed systems depth is concentrated in roles ending ~2006; current work is described primarily as full-stack product development and developer tooling rather than large-scale infrastructure ownership

Resume contains chronological inconsistencies (overlapping and improbable date ranges across multiple roles) that reduce confidence in the accuracy of the experience record and warrant direct clarification

key_factors.factor: AI-native tooling and agentic infrastructure experience
Distributed systems experience is real but dated
